content.text:
<think>
Looking at all the charts in the image showing percent of total alcohol consumption by type from 1965-2015:

- **Costa Rica** stands out clearly. The spirits (blue) category dominated nearly 80-90% of consumption from 1965 through the late 1990s, then dropped dramatically and steeply around 2000-2005, falling to roughly 40-50% — a drop of ~40 percentage points in just a few years.

Compared to other countries:
- Chile shows a gradual, multi-decade decline in spirits
- China shows a gradual decline from ~1995 onward
- Cuba shows relatively stable spirits throughout
- Other countries show much more gradual transitions

Costa Rica's drop is by far the steepest and most sudden.
</think>

<answer>
Costa Rica. The chart shows spirits (blue) dominating nearly 80-90% of its alcohol consumption from 1965 through the late 1990s, then plummeting dramatically around 2000-2005 to roughly 40-50% — a drop of approximately 40 percentage points in just a few years, making it by far the most sudden and steep decline in spirits consumption among the countries shown.
</answer>
